text.title: strict-mode-required-fields Procedure
text.summary: Five-step migration procedure for a Pydantic model going under OpenAI / Anthropic / Azure / Gemini strict mode.

step.name: scan-models
step.action: Walk every `BaseModel` subclass. For each model, check: presence of `ConfigDict(extra="forbid")`, fields with `Optional[X]` or default values that drop them from `required`, sentinel encodings (`""`, `-1`), nested `dict[str, Any]` fields.
step.output: List of violations matching the closed-enum `code` values in `02-output-contract.xml`.

step.name: add-extra-forbid
step.action: Insert `model_config = ConfigDict(extra="forbid")` at the top of every offending class. Propagate to all nested models referenced via Pydantic field types.
step.output: Patched files with `extra="forbid"` everywhere in the strict-mode tree.
step.decision-gate: If a model has a legitimate metadata bag (`dict[str, Any]`) — escalate to step 4.

step.name: convert-optional-to-nullable
step.action: Rewrite as `field_name: X | None = None`. Remove sentinel defaults. Update downstream consumers to handle the `None` case explicitly (no `if x:` truthiness shortcuts).

step.name: split-or-drop-metadata-bag
step.input: Models with `dict[str, Any]` or unconstrained nested objects.
step.action: Decide between: (a) split into typed sub-object + audit field, (b) drop strict mode for this call and use loose JSON mode, (c) keep strict and require the model to pick from a closed-key set.
step.output: One of: typed sub-object + `audit_blob: str`, decision record to use loose mode, or closed-key sub-schema.
step.decision-gate: If neither (a) nor (c) fits, document the (b) decision in the model's docstring with rationale.

step.name: add-ci-assert
step.action: Add a CI test that calls `assert_strict_schema(Model)` for every model used in a strict-mode call. The walker fails the build if any object node has missing `additionalProperties: false` or `required != properties`.
step.output: CI test file `tests/test_strict_mode.py` running the walker on the full strict-mode model set.
step.decision-gate: If a model legitimately needs `additionalProperties: true`, the model is in the wrong list — remove from strict-mode set instead of weakening the CI walker.
